useful_expressions.usage_zh: 面试开场时的自我介绍用语。
useful_expressions.usage_en: Used to start a self-introduction in an interview.

useful_expressions.zh: 我的优势是问题解决能力。
useful_expressions.usage_zh: 在面试中说明自己的核心能力。
useful_expressions.usage_en: State your core competency during an interview.

useful_expressions.en: I will adapt quickly after joining.
useful_expressions.usage_en: Show a proactive attitude toward your new role.

interview_tips_en: Connect your experience to the role.
interview_tips_zh: 准备简洁的 STAR 案例。
使用礼貌但自信的语句结尾。
把你的经验与岗位要求联系起来。

business_vocabulary.note_zh: 韩国面试通常包括个人介绍、能力展示和行为问题。
business_vocabulary.note_en: Korean interviews typically include self-introduction, competency demonstration, and behavioral questions.

business_vocabulary.note_zh: 面试中常被问到 "당신의 강점은 무엇인가요？"
business_vocabulary.note_en: Common interview question: 'What is your strength?'

business_vocabulary.note_zh: 入职后快速适应是韩国职场的重视品质。
business_vocabulary.note_en: Quick adaptation is highly valued in Korean workplaces.

sample_conversation.speaker: 求职者
sample_conversation.ko: 안녕하세요. 데이터 분석 직무에 지원한 김민수입니다.
sample_conversation.zh: 您好，我是申请数据分析岗位的 Kim Minsu。
sample_conversation.en: Hello, I am Kim Minsu, applying for the data analyst position.
sample_conversation.romanization: annyeonghaseyo. deiteo bunseok jingmue jiwonhan Kim Minsuimnida.

sample_conversation.speaker: 面试官
sample_conversation.ko: 이 직무에 관심을 가진 이유는 무엇인가요?
sample_conversation.zh: 你对这个岗位感兴趣的原因是什么？
sample_conversation.en: What made you interested in this role?
sample_conversation.romanization: i jingmue gwansimeul gajin iyuneun mueos-ingayo?

useful_expressions.zh: 我附上了工作经历说明。
useful_expressions.en: I have attached my career description.
useful_expressions.usage_zh: 发送简历和申请材料时使用。
useful_expressions.usage_en: Used when submitting your resume and career description.

useful_expressions.zh: 我以项目经验为中心编写了简历。
useful_expressions.en: I wrote it focusing on my project experience.
useful_expressions.usage_zh: 说明简历内容侧重项目经历。
useful_expressions.usage_en: Explain that your resume emphasizes hands-on project experience.

useful_expressions.usage_zh: 列出与岗位相关的资质证书。
useful_expressions.usage_en: List certifications relevant to the position.

interview_tips_en: Match keywords from the job description.
interview_tips_zh: 韩文简历的描述要具体。
与岗位描述的关键词保持一致。

business_vocabulary.note_zh: 韩国简历通常包括照片和个人信息。
business_vocabulary.note_en: Korean resumes typically include a photo and personal details.

business_vocabulary.note_zh: 比简历更详细地描述每段工作经历。
business_vocabulary.note_en: A detailed career description document, more thorough than a resume.

business_vocabulary.note_zh: 韩国职场非常看重各类资格证。
business_vocabulary.note_en: Certifications are highly valued in the Korean job market.

sample_conversation.speaker: 求职者
sample_conversation.zh: 我已经提交了简历和工作经历说明。
sample_conversation.en: I have submitted my resume and career description.

sample_conversation.speaker: HR
sample_conversation.ko: 포트폴리오도 함께 보내 주실 수 있나요?
sample_conversation.zh: 能把作品集也一起发给我吗？
sample_conversation.en: Could you also send your portfolio?
sample_conversation.romanization: poteupolliodo hamkke bonae jusil su innayo?

sample_conversation.speaker: 求职者
sample_conversation.en: Yes, I will send it to you by today.

useful_expressions.en: I will share it after confirming.
useful_expressions.usage_zh: 表示需要先确认再回复。
useful_expressions.usage_en: Indicate you need to confirm before responding.

useful_expressions.en: I have booked the meeting room.
useful_expressions.usage_zh: 通知同事会议室已预订。
useful_expressions.usage_en: Notify colleagues that the meeting room is booked.

useful_expressions.en: I will update the progress status.
useful_expressions.usage_zh: 在项目会议或定期汇报中使用。
useful_expressions.usage_en: Used in project meetings or regular status updates.

interview_tips_en: Avoid overly casual phrasing at first.
interview_tips_zh: 明确责任人和截止时间。
初期避免过于随意的表达。

business_vocabulary.note_zh: 职场中常用于分享资料或信息。
business_vocabulary.note_en: Commonly used in the workplace for sharing documents or information.

business_vocabulary.note_zh: 汇报工作时常用的表达。
business_vocabulary.note_en: Common expression when reporting on work progress.

business_vocabulary.note_zh: 韩国公司通常需要提前预约会议室。
business_vocabulary.note_en: Korean companies typically require advance booking for meeting rooms.

sample_conversation.speaker: 我
sample_conversation.en: I'll confirm and share it this afternoon.

useful_expressions.en: There are three agenda items today.
useful_expressions.usage_zh: 在会议开始时说明议程。
useful_expressions.usage_en: State the agenda at the beginning of a meeting.

useful_expressions.usage_zh: 在会议上礼貌地表达观点。
useful_expressions.usage_en: Politely express your opinion in a meeting.

useful_expressions.usage_zh: 在会议结束时进行总结。
useful_expressions.usage_en: Summarize the conclusion at the end of a meeting.

interview_tips_zh: 有不同意见时可以委婉表达。

business_vocabulary.note_zh: 韩国公司会议通常有书面议程。
business_vocabulary.note_en: Korean company meetings typically have written agendas.

business_vocabulary.note_zh: 会议中常用 "의견이 있으신가요？" 询问意见。
business_vocabulary.note_en: Common phrase: 'Does anyone have an opinion?'

business_vocabulary.note_zh: 会议结束时确认结论和行动项。
business_vocabulary.note_en: Confirm conclusions and action items at the end of meetings.

sample_conversation.speaker: 我
sample_conversation.en: Yes, I'd like to share my thoughts.

sample_conversation.speaker: 组长
sample_conversation.zh: 好的，我们进入下一个议题。
sample_conversation.en: Good. Let's move to the next item.

useful_expressions.usage_zh: 邮件中请求对方确认某事。
useful_expressions.usage_en: A polite way to request confirmation in an email.

useful_expressions.usage_zh: 邮件中提示对方查看附件。
useful_expressions.usage_en: Politely ask the recipient to check the attached file.

useful_expressions.en: I will wait for your reply.
useful_expressions.usage_zh: 邮件结尾表示等待回复。
useful_expressions.usage_en: Polite email closing indicating you await a response.

business_vocabulary.note_zh: 韩国职场邮件中用 첨부 表示附件。
business_vocabulary.note_en: In Korean workplace emails, '첨부' refers to an attachment.

business_vocabulary.note_zh: 职场中最常用的词汇之一。
business_vocabulary.note_en: One of the most commonly used words in the Korean workplace.
'답변 기다리겠습니다' means 'I will wait for your reply'.

sample_conversation.speaker: 我
sample_conversation.zh: 资料已通过邮件发送给您。
sample_conversation.en: I have sent the materials via email.

sample_conversation.speaker: 对方
sample_conversation.en: OK, I will confirm and get back to you.

useful_expressions.en: Is it convenient to talk now?
useful_expressions.usage_zh: 打电话先确认对方是否方便。
useful_expressions.usage_en: Ask if it's a good time to talk before starting the call.

useful_expressions.en: Please connect me to the person in charge.
useful_expressions.usage_zh: 打电话到公司要求转接到相应部门。
useful_expressions.usage_en: Call a company and ask to be transferred to the relevant department.
Ask to leave a message when the person is unavailable.

interview_tips_zh: 数字和时间再确认一遍。
准备好开场和结束用语。

business_vocabulary.note_zh: 韩国职场电话中常问 "지금 통화 가능하세요？"
business_vocabulary.note_en: Common workplace phone phrase: 'Are you available to talk now?'

business_vocabulary.note_zh: 打电话到公司时常用请求词。
business_vocabulary.note_en: Commonly used when calling a company.

business_vocabulary.note_zh: 请求转接到相应部门或人员。
business_vocabulary.note_en: Request to be connected to the relevant department or person.

sample_conversation.speaker: 我
sample_conversation.zh: 您好，请转接相关负责人。
sample_conversation.en: Hello, please connect me to the person in charge.

sample_conversation.speaker: 职员
sample_conversation.en: May I ask what this is regarding?
